title: Committed review prefers direct HTTPS PR-files retrieval for explicit PR numbers

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark that explicit PR-number committed review goes straight to the preferred direct shell-native HTTPS PR-files request before trying other PR metadata tooling or any CLI fallback.
provenance.genericCondition: Sanitized fixture models the simpler explicit-PR path where the direct shell-native HTTPS GitHub PR-files request should be the first scope-resolution attempt, avoiding avoidable detours through summary-only or spill-path metadata tools and avoiding automatic local `gh` fallback.

description: A committed-review scenario where an explicit PR number is supplied. The correct review should try the preferred direct shell-native HTTPS GitHub PR-files request first, instead of starting with active or viewed PR metadata tools that may only yield summary output or forbidden spill-file transports, and it must not auto-fallback to the local `gh` binary.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models the simpler explicit-PR path where the direct shell-native HTTPS GitHub PR-files request should be the first scope-resolution attempt, avoiding avoidable detours through summary-only or spill-path metadata tools and avoiding automatic local `gh` fallback.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should treat an explicit PR number as a prompt to try the preferred direct shell-native HTTPS GitHub PR-files request first, before active/viewed PR metadata tools or any CLI fallback.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not start explicit-PR scope resolution with active/viewed PR metadata tools while the preferred direct non-CLI PR-files API path for that same PR number remains available.
The review must not auto-fallback to the local `gh` binary for PR file retrieval when the user did not explicitly ask to use `gh`.

notes: Use this case to benchmark that explicit PR-number committed review goes straight to the preferred direct shell-native HTTPS PR-files request before trying other PR metadata tooling or any CLI fallback.
